name: American Persimmon
summary: Late-fall mast tree that fills the November-December food gap when other wildlife food is gone. Native range reaches southern PA; hardy to Zone 4 (–30°F) — easily survives Pocono winters. A canonical climate-winner expected to expand northward.
notes: Larval host for the spectacular Luna Moth and several hairstreaks. Sweet 1-inch orange fruit hangs after leaf-drop, drawing fox, raccoon, opossum, deer, turkey, and songbirds at a time of year when little else is available. Dioecious — plant clusters of 5+ for reliable fruiting. Distinctive blocky black bark texture is unmistakable in winter.

planting.aspect: Open old-field edges; openings; gap interiors
planting.light: Full sun preferred; tolerates partial shade
planting.soil: Acidic to neutral; well-drained; tolerates poor soils
planting.site_notes: Dioecious — plant in cluster of 5+ to ensure both male and female trees. Standard tube protection for the first 3–5 years. Climate-adapted species worth a trial planting in a single cluster.
